content: Verifying that the ninoscreens main window refactor for 3 pages of 12 tiles plus a RUN workspace was written completely. If the file is still truncated or broken, the next step is to rewrite it cleanly before reporting step 1 as complete.

User approved moving to step 2 in ninoscreens. Next action is to replace the temporary page navigation with the compact 36-button top matrix in 3 rows plus a dedicated RUN control, wiring button presses to pages 1-3 and the Run workspace while preserving the new foundation.

Beginning the actual step-2 implementation in ninoscreens: build the compact 36-slot top matrix (in 3 rows of 12) with a dedicated RUN control, keep buttons small and rectangular, and preserve the audio/API/fullscreen controls without crowding the interface.

User approved continuing after the compact 36+RUN matrix landing in code. Next work is to make the matrix live: sync the 36 buttons with tile state so the active slot, loaded slots, loading slots, and error/stuck slots are visually distinct, preparing for the later memory-gauge colors and contour states.

User approved continuing in the same direction. Next work is to make the 36-slot matrix match the planned contour semantics: green when a slot is ready/loaded, yallow when it is working/loading, red when it is in error or stuck, while keeping the interior fill av ailable for the later memory-gauge.

User approved launching the real memory gauge. Next work is to expose a per-tile memory metric from the web tiles or their processes, refresh it periodically, and map it to fill color states inside the 36-slot matrix using the planned thresholds (0-200MB, 200-700MB, 700MB+).
